title: Trachyandra glandulosa
taxonomy: Trachyandra glandulosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as the asphodels or lilyturf. This family has undergone several reclassifications over time, but it is now widely accepted that Asphodelaceae is a distinct and natural group. Trachyandra glandulosa is further classified within the subfamily Asphodeloideae, which consists of plants that are primarily found in southern Africa. The genus Trachyandra itself is a group of plants that are characterized by their unique inflorescences and leaf morphology. Within the genus Trachyandra, Trachyandra glandulosa is one of the many species that exhibit a range of adaptations to their environments.

intro: Trachyandra glandulosa, also known as the glandular Trachyandra or the glandular grass-lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Xanthorrhoeaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in South African medicine, and its extracts have been shown to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Trachyandra glandulosa include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Trachyandra glandulosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
